ORDER OVERRULING PLAINTIFF JONATHAN GRIFFITHS' DEMURRER TO AON CORPORATION AND AON RISK SERVICES WEST, INC.'S AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, MOTION TO STRIKE

LAW AND MOTION 302, CROSS DEFENDANT JONATHAN GRIFFITHS' DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED. AS TO THE EIGHTH CAUSE OF ACTION, DEFENDANT STATES A CAUSE OF ACTION FOR CONVERSION. PLAINTIFF CANNOT MAKE A DEMURRER TO PART OF A CAUSE OF ACTION. DEFENDANT'S ALLEGATION THAT PLAINTIFF SUBMITTED FALSE EXPENSE REPORTS PLEADS A SPECIFIC SUM CAPABLE OF IDENTIFICATION. (FIRST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT "FACC", 81. THE COURT OVERRULES THE DEMURRERS TO THE REMAINING CAUSES OF ACTION (2ND, 3RD, 4TH, 5TH). THE CALIFORNIA UNIFORM TRADE SECRETS ACT ("CUTSA") DOES NOT PREEMPT THOSE CAUSES OF ACTION BECAUSE THOSE CLAIMS ALLEGE A BROADER SPECTRUM OF MISCONDUCT THAT IS NOT BASED ON THE SAME NUCLEUS OF FACTS AS THE MISAPPROPRIATION CAUSE OF ACTION. THE COURT DENIES THE MOTION TO STRIKE. PLAINTIFF FAILED TO COMPLY WITH CRC 3.1322(A). ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR #6185

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF JONATHAN GRIFFITHS' MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS IS DENIED. FIRST, THE MOTION SEEKS A RULING REGARDING WHETHER PARAGRAPH 8(A)(III) OF THE PARTIES' CONTRACT IS ENFORCEABLE, BUT THE COMPLAINT DOES NOT CLEARLY REFER TO THAT PROVISION. AS A SEPARATE BASIS FOR DENYING THE MOTION, CCP 438(C)(1)(A) PROVIDES THAT THE COURT CAN GRANT JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS IN FAVOR OF THE PLAINTIFF: IF THE MOVING PARTY IS A PLAINTIFF, THAT THE COMPLAINT STATES FACTS SUFFICIENT TO CONSTITUTE A CAUSE OR CAUSES OF ACTION AGAINST THE DEFENDANT AND THE ANSWER DOES NOT STATE FACTS SUFFICIENT TO CONSTITUTE A DEFENSE TO THE COMPLAINT. (EMPHASIS ADDED). IN THIS CASE, DEFENDANT'S ANSWER DENIES ALL OF PLAINTIFF'S ALLEGATIONS AND PLAINTIFF FAILS TO ADDRESS DEFENDANT'S DEFENSES. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR #6185

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-14-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Cross Defendant JONATHAN GRIFFITHS' DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT is overruled. As to the eighth cause of action, Defendant states a cause of action for conversion. Plaintiff cannot make a demurrer to part of a cause of action. Defendant's allegation that plaintiff submitted false expense reports pleads a specific sum capable of identification. (First Amended Cross Complaint "FACC", 81. The Court overrules the demurrers to the remaining causes of action (2nd, 3rd, 4th, 5th). The California Uniform Trade Secrets Act ("CUTSA") does not preempt those causes of action because those claims allege a broader spectrum of misconduct that is not based on the same nucleus of facts as the misappropriation cause of action. The Court denies the motion to strike. Plaintiff failed to comply with CRC 3.1322(a). Order signed in open court. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR #6185

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
Off Calendar Nov-14-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Plaintiff JONATHAN GRIFFITHS' Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings is denied. First, the motion seeks a ruling regarding whether paragraph 8(a)(iii) of the parties' contract is enforceable, but the Complaint does not clearly refer to that provision. As a separate basis for denying the motion, CCP 438(c)(1)(A) provides that the Court can grant judgment on the pleadings in favor of the plaintiff: If the moving party is a plaintiff, that the complaint states facts sufficient to constitute a cause or causes of action against the defendant and the answer does not state facts sufficient to constitute a defense to the complaint. (emphasis added).
In this case, defendant's answer denies all of plaintiff's allegations and plaintiff fails to address defendant's defenses. Order signed in open court. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR #6185
